What if we put a price on food's carbon?

Set a global greenhouse-gas price - and, under a flexible diet, how much a year of healthy life is worth - and watch the world's croplands, diets, animal feed, emissions and health burden reorganise as the GLADE model re-optimises the food system. Everything updates live from a neural-network surrogate of the model.

Carbon price 0 USD / t CO2-eq
Value of a life-year 0 USD / year of life
– million years of life lost / yr
Advanced: other model uncertainties held at central estimates · click to adjust
Cropland use colour = main crop group · fade = cropland per land area
Pasture & grazing land shade = pasture per land area
lessmore
Net food-system emissions
0Gt CO2-eq / yr
System cost USD trillion / yr · the components summed
Global diet g / person / day · plant-based → animal-based
Animal feed Mt dry matter / yr · by source

Constant vs flexible diet. Under constant diet, consumption is pinned to the 2020 baseline; production, land use, animal feed and emissions still re-optimise as the price rises. Under flexible diet, the diet also re-optimises, shifting in response to the carbon price alone (health is left unvalued).

Net emissions. All greenhouse gases (CO2, CH4, N2O) as CO2-equivalent, including land-use change and sequestration credits from spared land.

Diet & feed. Diet is the global-average per-capita intake, shown by mass (g/person/day) or food energy (kcal/person/day) via the toggle; animal feed is total dry matter (Mt/yr) by source.

System cost. The model objective, split into terms that sum to it: production cost (growing, processing, trade and supplying food); resistance to change (penalties for deviating from observed production, plus -- under flexible diet -- the loss of consumer value relative to the starting diet); the social cost of carbon (the carbon price times net emissions, which turns negative as the system reaches net-negative emissions and earns sequestration credits); and, under flexible diet, the health burden (the value of a life-year times the diet-attributable years of life lost).

Health. Under flexible diet, the model trades diet cost against diet-related health: the readout shows the diet-attributable years of life lost (million YLL / yr), and the value-of-a-life-year dial sets how strongly that burden is priced into the optimisation. With a constant diet, health is not valued, so the dial and readout are inactive.

Maps. Cropland is coloured by its dominant crop group and faded by cropland area per unit land; pasture is shaded by grazing-land area per unit land.

Method. Values come from a multilayer-perceptron surrogate trained on a large global sensitivity ensemble of GLADE solves, evaluated live in your browser as you move the dials (no interpolation between precomputed scenarios). Non-dial uncertainties are held at their central estimates.

GLADE · global food-system optimisation
